violations.text: Walk-in cooler gasket was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Baseboards under 3 bay sink and area around greasetrap was observed with an accumulation of debris. The physical facilities to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Cheese sauces was observed held at 80-92 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were heated to manufacturer's minimum requirement temperature for hot holding. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
Gaskets for 3 door prep units were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2025

No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Per PIC, individual is scheduled to take food safety course and test in the following week. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. State guidance provided to PIC during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024
All handsinks in the kitchen area and prep area were lacking the proper notification signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). New chemical bottle was placed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024
